CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value1 INT, value2 INT);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 91, 15);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (2, 0, 0);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (3, -1, 1);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (4, 7, 8);
SELECT value1 & value2 FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT value1 & value2 AS result FROM test_table WHERE id = 2;
SELECT (value1 & value2) + 5 AS adjusted_result FROM test_table WHERE id = 3;
SELECT (value1 & value2) AS and_result, (value1 | value2) AS or_result FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, related_id INT);
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, 1), (2, 3);
SELECT t.id, (t.value1 & t.value2) AS and_result FROM test_table t JOIN another_table a ON t.id = a.related_id;
SELECT t1.value1, t2.value2, (t1.value1 & t2.value2) AS combined_result FROM test_table t1, test_table t2 WHERE t1.id < t2.id;
SELECT id, value1, value2, (value1 & (SELECT MAX(value1) FROM test_table)) AS max_and FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE boundary_cases (decimal_value DECIMAL);
INSERT INTO boundary_cases VALUES (0.0), (-1.0), (1.0);
SELECT id, (value1 & (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM boundary_cases)) AS boundary_test FROM test_table;
SELECT (SELECT MAX(value1) FROM test_table WHERE value2 > 0) & (SELECT MIN(value1) FROM test_table WHERE value2 < 10);
SELECT (SELECT AVG(value1) FROM test_table) & (SELECT AVG(value2) FROM test_table);
SELECT CASE WHEN (value1 & value2) = 0 THEN 'No Overlap' ELSE 'Has Overlap' END AS overlap_status FROM test_table;
